current_worker_checkpoint_blockers: full cargo test and clippy not rerun after curation

method.small: Actual local route benchmark. DX-WWW uses its Rust demo runtime; Astro/Svelte/HTMX use static local servers; Next.js uses next start.
method.medium: Scale payload/access model over docs and marketing-card page graphs from binary-web-lab. This is compiler evidence, not a full browser Lighthouse run.
method.big: Scale payload/access model over a 1200-row dashboard. DX-WWW uses adaptive viewport delivery for initial load and records full-data packet numbers separately.
method.score_formula: Overall = 25% small actual route + 20% medium scale + 20% big scale + 20% current product readiness + 15% developer experience. Community/adoption is not included.

DX-WWW.model: DX template/data packets for repeated docs and marketing cards

Astro.model: static HTML baseline, Astro-like output shape

Svelte.model: static HTML payload plus measured Svelte runtime floor

HTMX.model: static HTML payload plus measured HTMX runtime floor

Next.js.model: static HTML payload plus measured Next/React runtime floor

DX-WWW.model: adaptive initial viewport packet; full data can stream separately

Svelte.model: full dashboard payload plus measured Svelte runtime floor

HTMX.model: full dashboard payload plus measured HTMX runtime floor

Next.js.model: full dashboard payload plus measured Next/React runtime floor

verdict.honest_note: DX-WWW is now winning the measured small route and the adaptive medium/big payload model, but its product-readiness score is intentionally lower because App Router coverage, dx build proof, and the DX-owned dev feedback and diagnostics surface still need production proof.
